name: huffman-coding-classic-example
notes: Tie-break rule: leaves ordered alphabetically by symbol get order 0..n-1; each merge pops the two globally-smallest (freq,order) nodes and creates a new node with a fresh, always-larger order. First frequency set is the well-known Wikipedia Huffman example (weighted avg length 2.24 bits/symbol). Certificate isPrefixFree is also checked.
